name: restat-skills
description: Agent skill stack for manuscripts targeted at The Review of Economics and Statistics (REStat) — the venerable MIT Press journal edited at the Harvard Kennedy School (founded 1917/1919) for applied economics and applied econometrics. The pack is empirical-first and measurement-aware: it routes a manuscript from venue fit and credible causal identification, through robustness, exhibits, and house-style writing, into REStat's signature Data and Code Availability Policy — a replication package deposited to the journal's Harvard Dataverse and checked for reproducibility — and on through Editorial Express submission, referee strategy, and the R&R rebuttal. Twelve role skills span workflow routing, topic selection, literature positioning, identification, theory-model right-sizing, robustness, tables and figures, writing style, the replication package, referee strategy, submission preflight, and rebuttal. Bilingual en / zh-CN docs; Stata / R / Python conventions for applied causal-inference work.
